title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: For every graph $H$ there exists $c > 0$ such that $\kappa(G) \geq |G|^c$ for every $H$-free graph $G$.
context_text: Proposed by Erdős and Hajnal in 1977, this conjecture asserts that forbidding any fixed induced subgraph $H$ forces the existence of a clique or stable set of polynomial size. Despite considerable attention it is known only for very restricted families of $H$; the best general lower bound prior to this paper was $\kappa(G)\geq 2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}}$.
paper_contribution: Proves that every $H$-free graph $G$ with $|G|\geq 2$ satisfies $\kappa(G)\geq 2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}\,\log\log|G|}$ (Theorem 1.3), the first improvement over the $2^{c\sqrt{\log|G|}}$ bound for general $H$ since 1977, obtained as a corollary of a stronger quantitative result (Theorem 1.8) that also strengthens theorems of Rödl, Nikiforov, and Fox–Sudakov.
notes: PDF source — math reconstructed from readable PDF text; statement is unambiguous.
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. I. A loglog step towards Erdos-Hajnal

title: Fox–Sudakov polynomial density conjecture
statement_text: In Rödl's theorem (Theorem 1.4), the parameter $\delta$ can be taken to be a polynomial function of $x$ (for every fixed $H$).
context_text: Fox and Sudakov conjectured that the density parameter $\delta$ guaranteeing a large $x$-sparse or $x$-dense induced subgraph in every $H$-free graph can be chosen polynomially in $x$, and noted this would imply the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture. Prior to this paper the best bound was $\delta = 2^{-(\log(1/x))^2 \cdot c/|H|}$ (Fox–Sudakov, Theorem 1.5/1.7).
paper_contribution: Improves the exponent to $\delta = 2^{-c(\log(1/x))^2/\log\log(1/x)}$ via Theorem 1.8, a loglog improvement over the Fox–Sudakov bound, but does not achieve the conjectured polynomial bound.
notes: Conjecture stated in prose only (no labelled theorem environment); PDF source.
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. I. A loglog step towards Erdos-Hajnal

title: Conlon–Fox–Sudakov sparse pair conjecture (Conjecture 2.2)
statement_text: For every graph $H$ there exist $c_1, c_2 > 0$ such that for every $H$-free graph $G$ with $|G| \geq 2$, and all $x \in (0, 1/2)$, there exist disjoint $A, B \subseteq V(G)$ with $|A|, |B| \geq x^{c_1}|G|^{c_2}$ such that $B$ is $x$-sparse to $A$ in one of $G, \bar{G}$.
context_text: This conjecture of Conlon, Fox, and Sudakov strengthens a theorem of Erdős and Hajnal (Theorem 2.1) on the existence of large sparse pairs in $H$-free graphs. As the authors observe, the inductive argument used in the Erdős–Hajnal proof of 1.2 would yield Theorem 1.3 directly if this conjecture were true (by choosing $x = 1/(2\mu(G))$).
paper_contribution: The paper takes a different approach via blockades and leaves this conjecture open, proving the same conclusion (Theorem 1.3) by other means without establishing the conjecture.
notes: PDF source — statement reconstructed from readable PDF text; labeled Conjecture 2.2 in the paper.
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. I. A loglog step towards Erdos-Hajnal
